content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes an integer **T** for number of test cases as input, then for each test case reads an integer **N** on next **T** lines, and prints **N + 1** for each test case.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N+1 on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=3. The three N values are 4, 2 and -1. Adding one gives 5, 3 and 0 respectively.
Single test case with N=0, output is 0+1=1.
First N is the minimum allowed value, -10^9, so output is -10^9+1. Second N is the maximum allowed value, 10^9, so output is 10^9+1.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. Loop T times, each time read N, compute N+1 using 64‑bit integer arithmetic to avoid overflow, and output the result. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
